SUMMARY: NESTED | FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD DETAIL: FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD

javax.media.mscontrol.networkconnection
Class CodecPolicy

java.lang.Object
  javax.media.mscontrol.networkconnection.CodecPolicy

public class CodecPolicy
extends java.lang.Object

Allow the application to influence on the codecs and media-types used in the network transport.
The application intention is expressed by verbs like "prefer", "require", "exclude".
The CodecPolicy is set with SdpPortManager.setCodecPolicy(CodecPolicy), and will be enforced (later) when the connection is setup.

Operation

Each codec has a status, among the following:
  • allowed
  • required
  • excluded
Initially, all codecs have the allowed status, and all getters return an empty array.
The status of a codec can be changed using the setters of this class.

The application of the codec policy will cause a connection setup failure if:

  • an excluded codec must be used (or else, the media-type would have no available codec)
  • a required codec is not available
  • all the available media-types are excluded by the policy
On top of this, the application can indicate preferences, by an ordered list of codecs, see setCodecPreferences(String[]). The preferences are applied after all other constraints have been applied, and never cause a failure.

Notes:

  • The codec names are those described in RFC 3551 and associated RFCs. See also http://www.iana.org/assignments/rtp-parameters
  • All setter calls override the previous settings (no cumulative effect).
  • Some cases are not considered as an error by the JSR 309 spec, but may yet cause a setup failure from the media server or from the user agent:
    • Excluding both G.711 and telephone-event (some advanced codecs cannot encode inband DTMFs reliably)
    • The only usable codec is telephone-event

 void setCodecCapabilities(java.lang.String[] codecCapabilities)
          Set to to the excluded state all the codecs not mentioned in the argument.
 void setCodecPreferences(java.lang.String[] codecPreferences)
          This method indicates a preference between codecs.
 void setExcludedCodecs(java.lang.String[] excludedCodecs)
          Set to the excluded state all the given codecs.
 void setMediaTypeCapabilities(java.lang.String[] mediaTypeCapabilities)
          Set to to the excluded state all the media-types not mentioned in the argument.
 void setRequiredCodecs(java.lang.String[] requiredCodecs)
          Set to the required state all the given codecs.

Constructor Detail

CodecPolicy

public CodecPolicy()
Method Detail

getExcludedCodecs

public java.lang.String[] getExcludedCodecs()
Returns:
the excludedCodecs

setExcludedCodecs

public void setExcludedCodecs(java.lang.String[] excludedCodecs)
Set to the excluded state all the given codecs.

Example: if the application is not a "premium" service, it may want to disallow AMR-WB and MPEG4: 

 setExcludedCodecs(new String[]{ "AMR-WB", "MPEG4" });

getCodecCapabilities

public java.lang.String[] getCodecCapabilities()
Returns:
the codecCapabilities

setCodecCapabilities

public void setCodecCapabilities(java.lang.String[] codecCapabilities)
Set to to the excluded state all the codecs not mentioned in the argument.
The is like "exclude other codecs".

Example: If some terminal equipments in the network support only G.711, and possibly telephone-event: 

 setCodecCapabilities(new String[]{ "PCMU", "PCMA", "telephone-event" });
(video codecs are not impacted)

getRequiredCodecs

public java.lang.String[] getRequiredCodecs()
Returns:
the requiredCodecs

setRequiredCodecs

public void setRequiredCodecs(java.lang.String[] requiredCodecs)
Set to the required state all the given codecs.

Example: If some equipments in the network do not handle inband DTMFs: 

 setRequiredCodecs(new String[]{ "telephone-event" });

getCodecPreferences

public java.lang.String[] getCodecPreferences()
Returns:
the codecPreferences

setCodecPreferences

public void setCodecPreferences(java.lang.String[] codecPreferences)
This method indicates a preference between codecs. The order of the preference is the order of the array. Codecs that are not listed have a still lower preference.

Example: If the network bandwidth is large, using G.711 saves processing power: 

 setCodecPreferences(new String[]{ "PCMU", "PCMA" });

getMediaTypeCapabilities

public java.lang.String[] getMediaTypeCapabilities()
Returns:
the mediaTypeCapabilities

setMediaTypeCapabilities

public void setMediaTypeCapabilities(java.lang.String[] mediaTypeCapabilities)
Set to to the excluded state all the media-types not mentioned in the argument.
The is like "exclude other media".
